speaker: seller

args.message: Hey there! You've got great taste — this 2018 Camry LE is one of the cleanest examples I've seen come through here. Only 78,000 miles on Toyota's bulletproof 2.5L engine, just had a full service, and you know what they say about Camrys: these things run forever. I've got it priced at $18,495, which is fair for what you're getting, but let's talk!

speaker: buyer

args.message: Thanks for the pitch! A couple of quick questions: Has this Camry been in any accidents, even minor ones? And what kind of service history can you show me — is there documentation for that recent full service?

text: Thanks for the pitch! A couple of quick questions: Has this Camry been in any accidents, even minor ones? And what kind of service history can you show me — is there documentation for that recent full service?

speaker: seller

args.message: Great questions — I love a buyer who does their homework! No accidents on record, clean Carfax, you can see for yourself. As for the service history, absolutely, we've got documentation. The previous owner was meticulous — oil changes, tire rotations, the works. The most recent service was done right before it came to us, so you're good to go for thousands of miles. I can pull up the paperwork if you want to take a closer look, but trust me, this one's been babied. Anything else you'd like to know about it?

speaker: buyer

args.message: Sounds good. One more thing — the ad mentions it's "well-maintained," but I want to make sure: are there any mechanical issues, warning lights, or anything I should know about? And how long have you had it on the lot?

text: Sounds good. One more thing — the ad mentions it's "well-maintained," but I want to make sure: are there any mechanical issues, warning lights, or anything I should know about? And how long have you had it on the lot?

speaker: seller

args.message: No warning lights, no mechanical issues whatsoever — this thing runs like a dream. Starts right up, smooth transmission, AC blows cold, everything works exactly as it should. Toyota engineering at its finest, honestly. We've only had it on the lot about two weeks, and between you and me, I'm surprised it's still here. Cars like this usually move fast because people know the Camry's reputation. If you want, we can take it for a spin right now and you can hear that engine purr for yourself. You'll see what I mean — it drives like it just rolled off the assembly line.

speaker: buyer

args.message: I appreciate the confidence! Before we take it out, let me ask: what's your bottom line on price? I'm genuinely interested in this car, but I want to see if there's any room to negotiate from $18,495.

text: I appreciate the confidence! Before we take it out, let me ask: what's your bottom line on price? I'm genuinely interested in this car, but I want to see if there's any room to negotiate from $18,495.

speaker: seller

args.message: I appreciate you being upfront — that's how I like to do business too. Look, I'll be honest with you: at $18,495, we're already priced competitively. I saw a 2018 Camry LE with more miles go for $19,200 at auction just last week, so we're not trying to gouge anyone here. That said, I want to earn your business today. If you're serious and ready to move forward, I might have a little wiggle room — but I need to know you're committed. What kind of number were you thinking? Let's see if we can make something work.

That's $300 off our asking price, and you're driving away today in one of the most reliable sedans on the road with plenty of life left in it. What do you say?
